key_points: Some dorms still lack AC — confirmed issue since at least 1994
Communal bathrooms are standard; no private bathrooms in toured dorms
Dorm quality is inconsistent across campus buildings
key_quotes: I'm a graduate of the class of 1994 and you are telling me they STILL do NOT have Air Conditioning in every dorm?
The dormitory we visited didn't have private bathrooms—does this mean sharing communal bathrooms? That's concerning.
We got AC everywhere and it's adjustable in our rooms. And we don't pay extra for it.

key_points: Greek life exists and frat parties are mentioned positively by alumni
Small campus size creates a 'claustrophobic' feeling for some students
Strong sense of community and school spirit around 'Go Tribe' identity
key_quotes: The word 'claustrophobic' ran through my mind a few times after being accepted.
I partied so hard at W&M in 2000. Frat parties were awesome. Jon Stewart came through and got shit faced with us.
Go Tribe!! I will probably see you in the fall!

key_points: No substantive student data on dining quality found in sources
Off-campus food options exist along Richmond Road nearby
Limited firsthand complaints or praise — a data gap
key_quotes: Richmond road has lots of food options.
CONGRATULATIONS I recommend the cheese shop it's really good (it's a restaurant)

key_points: Academic rigor takes a real mental toll — confirmed by alumni and family
One commenter explicitly transferred out citing improved mental health elsewhere
High-pressure environment is consistent across decades of student accounts
key_quotes: It also takes a huge mental toll. Certainly a high pressure environment.

I'm a transfer student, and I just can't get over how different a big southern university is compared to W&M. Everyone seems sooo much happier and more carefree. I feel my mental health has drastically improved.
My anxiety reacted when you literally said the word 'claustrophobic.'

key_points: W&M gives little to no merit aid — multiple students chose cheaper schools instead
One student chose VCU over W&M to save $20k/year
Monroe Scholar award ($15k) is a notable exception for top admits
key_quotes: William and Mary gave me nothing and it would cost more than $35,000 per year.
We declined W&M and UVA 2 years ago in favor of VCU for the same reason.
I am committed to William & Mary as a Monroe Scholar!

key_points: Rigorous academics are the defining characteristic — universally acknowledged
Some professors have legendary GPA-crushing reputations; one made campus news
Strong alumni prep for grad school; medical school alumni praise the rigor
key_quotes: He was so legendary for crushing GPAs that the Flat Hat did a piece on his reputation as a GPA killer.
Because of the academic rigor, you will be very well prepared for graduate school.
At first I thought William and Mary was in the middle of nowhere until I started medical school in Kentucky. It's all about perspective.

key_points: Admissions office praised for personable, warm communication style
Increasing class sizes flagged as a growing concern by current students
Historical tension exists: student video documents a presidential resignation
key_quotes: W&M's email and mail advertising is awesome.
The increasing class sizes is a big big problem for me.
This was the student experience of Nichol's resignation. I'm so glad someone was recording our story.

key_points: Campus is genuinely historic and beautiful — archaeology happens on-site
Williamsburg feels small/rural to some but more accessible than it looks
Busch Gardens, beaches, Richmond all within 30-45 min; Target is 10 min
key_quotes: Truly wild how old some of the buildings on campus are. Not often you can have archeology going on at your campus.
There's plenty to do off campus. W&M is not in the middle of nowhere. There are beaches 5 miles away.
The heat here in VA, especially Williamsburg, can be really bad. Summer of 2022 it was 102-112 degrees for nearly the entire time.

key_points: Strongest pipeline is public service — school claims highest % of any US college
Strong alumni network consistently praised across decades of graduates
Less clear advantage for finance/consulting vs. a flagship like UVA
key_quotes: There is a very strong alumni network and, because of the academic rigor, you will be very well prepared for graduate school.
The school has the highest percent of students that go into public service.
Do you wanna do finance/consulting? I'm not sure W&M will give you a huge boost anyway.

key_points: OOS sticker price (~$55k/year) with minimal aid is a dealbreaker for many
In-state students get significantly better value; OOS value is questionable
Multiple students chose cheaper schools (VCU, LSU) without regret
key_quotes: LSU costs about 14k a year while William and Mary would cost closer to 55k.
Money. It was literally just money. VCU will cost me $15,000 per year. William and Mary gave me nothing.
Tons of my friends made the same decision and were SO happy with it.

key_points: Academically intense, historically rich, tight-knit — but not for everyone
Strong loyalty among alumni; current students often love it despite complaints
Best fit for public service-oriented, intellectually driven, community-minded students
key_quotes: I picked W&M over UVA 30 years ago — never regretted it because it was right for me.
I loved your video, it reminded me of why I loved W&M so much.
This sounds like the school of my dreams, or at least as close as it could be.

key_points: **Mental Health:** Transfer student reports drastic improvement after leaving W&M
**Housing:** No AC in some dorms is a decades-old unresolved infrastructure problem
**Financial Aid:** Near-zero merit aid for OOS students makes cost unsustainable for many
key_quotes: I feel my mental health has drastically improved [after transferring from W&M].
You are telling me they STILL do NOT have Air Conditioning in every dorm? — Class of 1994 alum
William and Mary gave me nothing and it would cost more than $35,000 per year.

key_points: Monroe Scholar program offers $15k and research opportunities for top admits
Williamsburg's historic district is literally walkable from campus
Admissions team is unusually warm — sends personalized poems and holiday emails
key_quotes: Whoever wrote this [W&M poem] needs a damn raise.
Committing to William & Mary as a Monroe Scholar! Go Tribe!
Not often you can have archaeology going on at your campus.
